/* Main banner container. Banner: background, border colour and width and adding a margin between banner and article header*/
.cnotice {
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
    border: 4px solid #374C87;
    border-radius: 14px;
    margin-bottom: 1em;
    cursor: pointer;
    color: #1B5E20;
    background: #FFF;
    font-weight: 500;
}

@media screen {
    html.skin-theme-clientpref-night .cnotice {
        background: none;
    }
}

@media screen and (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
    html.skin-theme-clientpref-os .cnotice {
        background: none;
    }
}

/* Sets the minimum banner height. If img + logo-container margins > height. Banner will be larger*/
#cnotice-main {
    display: table;
    width: 100%;
    height: 80px; /* need to set height for height 100% to work on elements within it */
}

/*MOBILE - Adjust mobile height of banner*/
body.skin-minerva #cnotice-main {
    height: 100px;
}

/*IMPORTANT - don't touch*/
.cnotice-message-container,
.cnotice-logo-container,
.cnotice-misc-container {
    display: table-cell;
    height: 100%;
    vertical-align: middle;
}

/* --- Main message --- */
.cnotice-message {
    position: relative;
    margin: 0;
    width: min(1000px, 95%);
    line-height: 1.2;
    padding: 5px 5px 5px 5px;
}

@media (min-width: 1200px) {
    .cnotice-message {
         padding: 15px 0px 11px 30px;
    }
}

body.rtl .cnotice-message {
    padding: 5px 5px 5px 5px;
}

.cnotice-message p {
    margin-inline: auto;
    color: var(--color-base,#202122);
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-weight: bold;
    font-size: 100%;
    opacity: 1;
    text-align: center;
    padding-top: 10px;
    padding-bottom: 10px;
}

}


/* --- Logo Image text --- */
.cnotice-logo-container {

    height: 38px;
    border-radius: 19px;
    vertical-align: middle;
}

#cnotice-toggle-box {
    cursor: pointer;
    position: absolute;
    top: 2px;
    right: 3px;
    z-index: 1;
    opacity: .8;
    background: #FFC803;
    border-radius: 8px;
}

body.rtl #cnotice-toggle-box {
    left: 3px;

}

#cnotice-toggle-box:hover { 
    opacity: 1;
}

#cnotice-toggle-icon{

    height: 100%;

<div class="shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux" style="display:none" about="#mwt3" typeof="mw:Transclusion" id="mwAw" data-mw="{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Short description","href":"./Template:Short_description"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Statistical tool to assess investments"}},"i":0}}]}">Statistical tool to assess investments</div><span class="mw-empty-elt" about="#mwt3"><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Articles_with_short_description"><link rel="mw:PageProp/Category" href="./Category:Short_description_matches_Wikidata"></span>

<p id="mwBA">The <b id="mwBQ">Deflated Sharpe ratio</b> (<b id="mwBg">DSR</b>) is a statistical method used to determine whether the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sharpe_ratio" title="Sharpe ratio" id="mwBw">Sharpe ratio</a> of an investment strategy is statistically significant, developed in 2014 by <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marcos_López_de_Prado?action=edit&redlink=1" title="Marcos López de Prado (page does not exist)" class="new" typeof="mw:LocalizedAttrs" id="mwCA" data-mw-i18n="{"title":{"lang":"x-page","key":"red-link-title","params":["Marcos López de Prado"]}}">Marcos López de Prado</a> at <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guggenheim_Partners" title="Guggenheim Partners" id="mwCQ">Guggenheim Partners</a> and <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cornell_University" title="Cornell University" id="mwCg">Cornell University</a>, and <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_H._Bailey_(mathematician)" title="David H. Bailey (mathematician)" id="mwCw">David H. Bailey</a> at <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lawrence_Berkeley_National_Laboratory" title="Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory" id="mwDA">Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory</a>. It corrects for <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Selection_bias" title="Selection bias" id="mwDQ">selection bias</a>, <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_dredging" title="Data dredging" id="mwDg">backtest overfitting</a>, sample length, and non-normality in return distributions, providing a more reliable test of financial performance, especially when many trials are evaluated.<sup about="#mwt4" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-DSR_1-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"DSR"}}"><a href="#cite_note-DSR-1" id="mwDw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwEA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwEQ">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwEg">]</span></span></a></sup> The application of the DSR, helps practitioners to detect false investment strategies.</p>

<p id="mwEw">DSR offers a more precise and robust adjustment for <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiple_comparisons_problem" title="Multiple comparisons problem" id="mwFA">multiple testing</a> compared to traditional methods like the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Šidák_correction" title="Šidák correction" id="mwFQ">Šidák correction</a> because it explicitly models both the selection bias arising from choosing the best among many trials and the estimation uncertainty inherent in Sharpe ratios. Unlike Šidák, which assumes independence and adjusts <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-value" title="P-value" id="mwFg">p-values</a> based only on the number of tests, the DSR accounts for the variance of Sharpe estimates, the number of trials, and their effective independence, often estimated through clustering. This leads to a more realistic threshold for statistical significance that reflects the true probability of a false discovery in data-mined environments. As a result, the DSR is particularly well-suited for finance, where researchers often conduct large-scale, correlated searches for profitable strategies without strong prior hypotheses.<sup about="#mwt5" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Detection_2-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Detection"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Detection-2" id="mwFw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwGA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwGQ">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwGg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt8" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-3"}}"><a href="#cite_note-3" id="mwGw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwHA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwHQ">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwHg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc" id="mwHw" data-mw="{"autoGenerated":true}"></section><section data-mw-section-id="1" id="mwIA" aria-labelledby="Relation_to_the_Sharpe_ratio"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Relation_to_the_Sharpe_ratio">Relation to the Sharpe ratio</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: Relation to the Sharpe ratio"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwIQ">One of the most important statistics for assessing the performance of an investment strategy is the <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sharpe_ratio" title="Sharpe ratio" id="mwIg">Sharpe ratio</a> (SR). The Sharpe ratio was developed by <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_F._Sharpe" title="William F. Sharpe" id="mwIw">William F. Sharpe</a> and is a widely used measure of risk-adjusted return, calculated as the annualized ratio of excess return over the risk-free rate to the standard deviation of returns. While useful, the Sharpe Ratio has important limitations, especially when applied to multiple strategy evaluations. Issues such as selection bias, where the best-performing strategy is chosen from a large set, and backtest overfitting, where a strategy is tailored to past data, can inflate the Sharpe Ratio, leading to misleading conclusions about a strategy's effectiveness. Additionally, the Sharpe Ratio assumes normally distributed returns,<sup about="#mwt11" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-4" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-4"}}"><a href="#cite_note-4" id="mwJA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwJQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJg">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJw">]</span></span></a></sup> an assumption often violated in practice, and it does not take into account sample length.<sup about="#mwt12" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-AMS_5-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"AMS"}}"><a href="#cite_note-AMS-5" id="mwKA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwKQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKg">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="2" id="mwLA" aria-labelledby="Applying_the_Deflated_Sharpe_ratio_in_practice"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Applying_the_Deflated_Sharpe_ratio_in_practice">Applying the Deflated Sharpe ratio in practice</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Applying the Deflated Sharpe ratio in practice"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

<section data-mw-section-id="3" id="mwLQ" aria-labelledby="1._Get_a_record_of_all_the_trials"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="1._Get_a_record_of_all_the_trials">1. Get a record of all the trials</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: 1. Get a record of all the trials"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwLg">In order to apply the DSR, researchers need to record the investment performance in returns (%), for every backtest that they ran. This is in relation to the development of a single specific strategy. For example: when building a momentum based strategy that trades at the end-of-day, 100 historical simulations were run to evaluate the performance and the best set of parameters were selected for the final strategy. Here all 100 simulations need to be recorded, with the strategies daily returns in<span typeof="mw:DisplaySpace" id="mwLw"> </span>%.</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="4" id="mwMA" aria-labelledby="2._Estimating_the_effective_number_of_trials_N"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="2._Estimating_the_effective_number_of_trials_N">2. Estimating the effective number of trials <i id="mwMQ">N</i></h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=4" title="Edit section: 2. Estimating the effective number of trials N"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwMg">In practice, many trials are not independent due to overlapping features. To estimate the effective number of independent trials <i id="mwMw">N</i>, López de Prado (2018) proposes three techniques to clustering similar strategies using <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unsupervised_learning" title="Unsupervised learning" id="mwNA">unsupervised learning</a> techniques:</p>

<ol id="mwNQ"><li id="mwNg">The Optimal Number of Clusters (ONC) algorithm.<sup about="#mwt13" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Detection_2-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Detection"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Detection-2" id="mwNw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwOA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwOQ">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwOg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt16" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:0_6-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:0-6"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:0-6" id="mwOw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwPA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPQ">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt19" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:1_7-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:1-7"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:1-7" id="mwPw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwQA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQQ">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQg">]</span></span></a></sup></li>
<li id="mwQw"><a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hierarchical_clustering" title="Hierarchical clustering" id="mwRA">Hierarchical clustering</a> could be used to get a conservative lower bound for <i id="mwRQ">N</i>.</li>
<li id="mwRg">Alternatively, spectral methods (e.g. eigenvalue distribution of the correlation matrix) can also provide estimates of <i id="mwRw">N</i>.<sup about="#mwt20" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Detection_2-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Detection"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Detection-2" id="mwSA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwSQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwSg">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwSw">]</span></span></a></sup></li></ol>
<p id="mwTA"><b id="mwTQ">Tip:</b></p>

<ul id="mwTg"><li id="mwTw">Multiple testing exercises should be carefully planned in advance, so as to avoid running an unnecessary large number of trials. Investment theory, not computational power, should motivate what experiments are worth conducting.<sup about="#mwt21" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-DSR_1-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"DSR"}}"><a href="#cite_note-DSR-1" id="mwUA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwUQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUg">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUw">]</span></span></a></sup></li></ul>

<p id="mwVA"><b id="mwVQ">Steps to estimate <i id="mwVg">N</i>:</b></p>

<p id="mwVw"><b id="mwWA">2.1.</b> Convert the correlation matrix to a distance matrix.</p>

<p id="mwWQ">In order to apply a clustering algorithm to the returns data, we need make use of a statistical association measure (such as a correlation matrix) and we need to transform it into a distance matrix (such as angular distance) so that elements that are very similar to each other will be close together in their higher-dimensional space.<sup about="#mwt24" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-8" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-8"}}"><a href="#cite_note-8" id="mwWg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwWw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXA">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt27" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-9" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-9"}}"><a href="#cite_note-9" id="mwXg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwXw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwYA">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwYQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<p id="mwYg"><b id="mwYw">2.2.</b> Apply a clustering algorithm to estimate the number of independent trials</p>

<p id="mwZA">The number of clusters <i id="mwZQ">N</i>, are an estimate of the number of independent trials.</p>

<p id="mwZg"><b id="mwZw">2.3</b> Plot the block correlation matrix</p>

<p id="mwaA">In the figure below we can see a correlation matrix before and after clustering has been applied. Note how we can see blocks down the diagonal, each block corresponds to a cluster.<sup about="#mwt28" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:1_7-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:1-7" id="mwaQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwag"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwaw">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwbA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<figure class="mw-halign-center" typeof="mw:File/Thumb" id="mwbQ"><a href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Correlation_before_and_after_clustering.png" class="mw-file-description" id="mwbg"><img resource="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Correlation_before_and_after_clustering.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Correlation_before_and_after_clustering.png/960px-Correlation_before_and_after_clustering.png" decoding="async" data-file-width="1526" data-file-height="598" data-file-type="bitmap" height="274" width="700" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b1/Correlation_before_and_after_clustering.png 2x" class="mw-file-element" id="mwbw"></a><figcaption id="mwcA">Clustering of 6,385 trials, typical of multiple testing of a group of strategies, before and after clustering.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwcQ"><b id="mwcg">Tip</b>: If you don't use the ONC algorithm to cluster, then you can have blocks with trials that don't match very closely. The ONC algorithm uses silhouette scores to make sure each trial is in the best cluster, at the expense of high computational complexity and longer run times.</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="5" id="mwcw" aria-labelledby="3._Compute_the_Sharpe_ratio_variance,_across_clusters"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="3._Compute_the_Sharpe_ratio_variance,_across_clusters"><span id="3._Compute_the_Sharpe_ratio_variance.2C_across_clusters" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>3. Compute the Sharpe ratio variance, across clusters</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=5" title="Edit section: 3. Compute the Sharpe ratio variance, across clusters"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwdA"><b id="mwdQ">3.1</b> Calculate the Sharpe ratio for each cluster.</p>

<p id="mwdg">Each cluster will now form a collection of time series returns (in%), for each cluster you need to create a new time series which represents that cluster using the Inverse Variance Portfolio (IVP) and then compute the Sharpe Ratio for each IVP portfolio. One doesn't need to use the IVP - the goal is to form an aggregate cluster return time series. For this a weighting scheme needs to be used, another alternative could be the minimum variance portfolio.<sup about="#mwt29" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:1_7-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:1-7" id="mwdw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mweA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mweQ">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mweg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<p id="mwew"><b id="mwfA">3.2</b> Compute the variance of these Sharpe ratios</p>

<p id="mwfQ"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt30" id="mwfg" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\mathbf{V}[\\hat{SR}_n]"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
          <mi mathvariant="bold">V</mi>
        </mrow>
        <mo stretchy="false">[</mo>
        <msub>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>
                  <mi>S</mi>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
              </mover>
            </mrow>
          </mrow>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mi>n</mi>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
        <mo stretchy="false">]</mo>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/982fa26cb95e80fad2f7571573cb453d4bf64296" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.838ex; width:7.795ex; height:3.343ex;" alt="{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}"></span> is used in the next step, where we apply the False Strategy Theorem to determine the Expected Maximum Sharpe ratio.</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="6" id="mwfw" aria-labelledby="4._Compute_the_expected_maximum_Sharpe_ratio_using_the_False_Strategy_Theorem"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="4._Compute_the_expected_maximum_Sharpe_ratio_using_the_False_Strategy_Theorem">4. Compute the expected maximum Sharpe ratio using the False Strategy Theorem</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=6" title="Edit section: 4. Compute the expected maximum Sharpe ratio using the False Strategy Theorem"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwgA">Using the equation from the False Strategy Theorem (FST)<sup about="#mwt31" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-FST_10-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"FST"}}"><a href="#cite_note-FST-10" id="mwgQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwgg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwgw">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhA">]</span></span></a></sup> we can compute <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt32" id="mwhQ" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0 "}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span>, which is the threshold Sharpe Ratio that reflects the highest Sharpe Ratio expected from <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt33" id="mwhg" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"N"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle N}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>N</mi>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle N}</annotation>

</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/f5e3890c981ae85503089652feb48b191b57aae3" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:2.064ex; height:2.176ex;" alt="{\displaystyle N}"></span> unskilled strategies.</p>

<p id="mwmQ"><b id="mwmg">Note:</b></p>

<p id="mwmw">The FST highlights that the optimal outcome of an unknown number of historical simulations is right-unbounded, with enough trials, there is no Sharpe ratio sufficiently large enough to reject the hypothesis that a strategy is false, i.e., that it is over-fit and wont generalize in the out-of-sample data.<sup about="#mwt41" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-AMS_5-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"AMS"}}"><a href="#cite_note-AMS-5" id="mwnA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwnQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwng">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwnw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt42" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:1_7-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:1-7" id="mwoA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwoQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwog">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwow">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<li id="mwug"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt51" id="mwuw" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\Phi"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \Phi }">

<p id="mwvQ"><b id="mwvg">Notes</b>:</p>

<ul id="mwvw"><li id="mwwA">Readers may recognize that the DSR is the Probabilistic Sharpe Ratio (PSR),<sup about="#mwt54" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:2_11-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:2-11"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:2-11" id="mwwQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwwg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwww">[</span>11<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwxA">]</span></span></a></sup> where <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt55" id="mwxQ" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span> is the maximum expected Sharpe Ratio (estimated using the False Strategy Theorem) instead of a simple threshold SR (often 0).</li>
<li id="mwxg">The PSR assumes that only 1 trial was run and is often used to determine that the observed SR is greater than 0.</li>
<li id="mwxw">To account for multiple-testing, use the DSR.</li>
<li id="mwyA">The DSR will increase with:
<ul id="mwyQ"><li id="mwyg">Greater observed SRs.</li>
<li id="mwyw">Longer track records.</li>
<li id="mwzA">Positively skewed returns.</li></ul></li>
<li id="mwzQ">The DSR decreases with:
<ul id="mwzg"><li id="mwzw">Fatter tails (Kurtosis).</li></ul></li></ul>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="8" id="mw0A" aria-labelledby="6._Complete_the_template_for_disclosing_multiple_tests"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="6._Complete_the_template_for_disclosing_multiple_tests">6. Complete the template for disclosing multiple tests</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=8" title="Edit section: 6. Complete the template for disclosing multiple tests"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mw0Q"><b id="mw0g">6.1</b> Aggregate statistics into a table.</p>

<p id="mw0w">Several peer reviewed papers recommend to aggregate the cluster statistics into a table format.<sup about="#mwt56" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:0_6-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:0-6" id="mw1A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw1Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1g">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1w">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt57" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-DataScience_12-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"DataScience"}}"><a href="#cite_note-DataScience-12" id="mw2A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw2Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw2g">[</span>12<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw2w">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt60" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-13" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-13"}}"><a href="#cite_note-13" id="mw3A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw3Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw3g">[</span>13<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw3w">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<p id="mw4A">The table below is Exhibit 7 from "A Practitioner’s Guide to the Optimal Number of Clusters Algorithm".<sup about="#mwt61" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:0_6-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:0-6" id="mw4Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw4g"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw4w">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw5A">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<figure class="mw-halign-center" typeof="mw:File/Frameless" id="mw5Q"><a href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Cluster_statistics.jpg" class="mw-file-description" title="Template for Disclosing Multiple Tests" id="mw5g"><img alt="Template for Disclosing Multiple Tests" resource="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Cluster_statistics.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/65/Cluster_statistics.jpg/960px-Cluster_statistics.jpg" decoding="async" data-file-width="1800" data-file-height="509" data-file-type="bitmap" height="226" width="800" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/6/65/Cluster_statistics.jpg 2x" class="mw-file-element" id="mw5w"></a><figcaption id="mw6A">Template for Disclosing Multiple Tests <sup about="#mwt62" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:0_6-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:0-6" id="mw6Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw6g"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw6w">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw7A">]</span></span></a></sup></figcaption></figure>
<p id="mw7Q">Where:</p>

<ul id="mw7g"><li id="mw7w">Cluster is the index of the cluster; there are <i id="mw8A">N</i> clusters.</li>
<li id="mw8Q">Strat Count is the number of strategies included in that cluster.</li>
<li id="mw8g">aSR is the annualized Sharpe Ratio of that cluster's inverse variance portfolio (IVP).</li>
<li id="mw8w">SR is the non-annualized Sharpe Ratio of that cluster's IVP.</li>
<li id="mw9A">Skew is the skew of the returns of that cluster's IVP.</li>
<li id="mw9Q">Kurt is the kurtosis of the returns of that cluster's IVP.</li>
<li id="mw9g">T is the number of observations in the cluster's IVP.</li>
<li id="mw9w">sqrt(V[SR]) is the square root of the variance of Sharpe Ratios that was computed in step 3.</li>
<li id="mw-A">E[max SR] is the Expected Maximum Sharpe ratio (<span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt63" id="mw-Q" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span>), computed in step 4.</li>
<li id="mw-g">DSR is the Deflated Sharpe Ratio for that cluster's IVP.</li></ul>

<p id="mw-w"><b id="mw_A">6.2</b> Plot the Sharpe Ratios, for each cluster.</p>
<figure class="mw-halign-center" typeof="mw:File/Frameless" id="mw_Q"><a href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:DSR_Bar_Plot.png" class="mw-file-description" title="Bar plot of Deflated Sharpe Ratios, for each cluster." id="mw_g"><img alt="Bar plot of Deflated Sharpe Ratios, for each cluster." resource="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:DSR_Bar_Plot.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d6/DSR_Bar_Plot.png/960px-DSR_Bar_Plot.png" decoding="async" data-file-width="1686" data-file-height="898" data-file-type="bitmap" height="320" width="600" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d6/DSR_Bar_Plot.png/1280px-DSR_Bar_Plot.png 2x" class="mw-file-element" id="mw_w"></a><figcaption id="mwAQA">Bar plot of Deflated Sharpe Ratios, for each cluster.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwAQE">In the figure above, we can see a collection of non-annualized Sharpe ratios for the 26 independent trials that were tested in the development of this investment strategy. The bars are highlighted based on if they passed the DSR at a 95% confidence level.</p>

<p id="mwAQI">Note that this bar chart doesn't correspond to table above in Exhibit 7 but shares the result that only 1 cluster passed the DSR. The goal with this analysis is to show that for all clusters, except 1 - all of them failed the DSR. This would indicate that the strategy is over-fit and is likely to be a false investment strategy.</p>

<p id="mwAQM"><b id="mwAQQ">6.3</b> Plot the cumulative returns of the strategies. </p>
<figure class="mw-halign-center" typeof="mw:File/Frameless" id="mwAQU"><a href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png" class="mw-file-description" title="ICumulative Returns of Trials" id="mwAQY"><img alt="ICumulative Returns of Trials" resource="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ca/Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png/500px-Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png" decoding="async" data-file-width="1086" data-file-height="826" data-file-type="bitmap" height="304" width="400" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ca/Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png/960px-Plot_Cumulative_Returns.png 2x" class="mw-file-element" id="mwAQc"></a><figcaption id="mwAQg">ICumulative Returns of Trials</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwAQk">In the figure above the cumulative returns are plotted. On the y axis is the total return in% and the x axis are the time indexes. Do you see the very straight line (the strategy with an outlier performance)?</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="9" id="mwAQo" aria-labelledby="7._Derive_a_conclusion_from_these_results"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="7._Derive_a_conclusion_from_these_results">7. Derive a conclusion from these results</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=9" title="Edit section: 7. Derive a conclusion from these results"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAQs">As seen in the plot of cumulative returns, there is one outlier strategy which is likely a false investment strategy as this outlier has very high performance relative to its own cluster and others.</p>

<p id="mwAQw">We can see in the bar plots that all the cluster portfolios failed to pass the DSR at a 95% confidence level, except for the one that included this outlier strategy.</p>

</section></section><section data-mw-section-id="10" id="mwAQ0" aria-labelledby="Mathematical_definitions"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Mathematical_definitions">Mathematical definitions</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=10" title="Edit section: Mathematical definitions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="12" id="mwATY" aria-labelledby="False_Strategy_Theorem:_statement_and_proof"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="False_Strategy_Theorem:_statement_and_proof">False Strategy Theorem: statement and proof</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=12" title="Edit section: False Strategy Theorem: statement and proof"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwATc">The <b id="mwATg">False Strategy Theorem</b> provides the theoretical foundation for the Deflated Sharpe Ratio (DSR) by quantifying how much the best Sharpe Ratio among many unskilled strategies is expected to exceed zero purely due to chance. Even if all tested strategies have true Sharpe Ratios of zero, the highest observed Sharpe Ratio will typically be positive and statistically significant—unless corrected. The DSR corrects for this inflation.<sup about="#mwt80" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-FST_10-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"FST"}}"><a href="#cite_note-FST-10" id="mwATk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATs">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<section data-mw-section-id="13" id="mwAT0" aria-labelledby="Statement"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Statement">Statement</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=13" title="Edit section: Statement"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAT4">Let <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt81" id="mwAT8" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\{\\hat{SR}_1, \\hat{SR}_2, \\dots, \\hat{SR}_N\\}"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \{{\hat {SR}}_{1},{\hat {SR}}_{2},\dots ,{\hat {SR}}_{N}\}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">

        <mo fence="false" stretchy="false">{</mo>

<p id="mwAVE">This value <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt90" id="mwAVI" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">

It represents a benchmark that any observed Sharpe Ratio must exceed in order to be considered statistically significant.</p>

</section></section><section data-mw-section-id="15" id="mwAWw" aria-labelledby="Implication_for_the_DSR"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Implication_for_the_DSR">Implication for the DSR</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=15" title="Edit section: Implication for the DSR"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

<p id="mwAW0">The False Strategy Theorem shows that in large-scale testing, even unskilled strategies will produce apparently "significant" Sharpe Ratios. To correct for this, the DSR adjusts the observed Sharpe Ratio <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt102" id="mwAW4" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\hat{SR}^*"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msup>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>
                  <mi>S</mi>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
              </mover>
            </mrow>
          </mrow>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
          </mrow>
        </msup>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/feb56d887721483fd15981dd21c1d43a7726c775" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:4.317ex; height:3.009ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}"></span> by subtracting the expected maximum from noise <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt103" id="mwAW8" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span>, and scaling by the standard error around the null hypothesis:</p>

<dl id="mwAXA"><dd id="mwAXE"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt104" id="mwAXI" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\text{DSR} = \\Phi \\left( \\frac{\\hat{SR}^* - SR_0}{\\sigma_{\\hat{SR_0}}} \\right)"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\text{DSR}}=\Phi \left({\frac {{\hat {SR}}^{*}-SR_{0}}{\sigma _{\hat {SR_{0}}}}}\right)}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
          <mtext>DSR</mtext>
        </mrow>
        <mo>=</mo>
        <mi mathvariant="normal">Φ<!-- Φ --></mi>
        <mrow>
          <mo>(</mo>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mfrac>
              <mrow>
                <msup>
                  <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                      <mover>
                        <mrow>
                          <mi>S</mi>
                          <mi>R</mi>
                        </mrow>
                        <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
                      </mover>
                    </mrow>
                  </mrow>
                  <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                    <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
                  </mrow>
                </msup>
                <mo>−<!-- − --></mo>
                <mi>S</mi>
                <msub>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                  <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                    <mn>0</mn>
                  </mrow>
                </msub>
              </mrow>
              <msub>
                <mi>σ<!-- σ --></mi>
                <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                  <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                    <mover>
                      <mrow>
                        <mi>S</mi>
                        <msub>
                          <mi>R</mi>
                          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                            <mn>0</mn>
                          </mrow>
                        </msub>
                      </mrow>
                      <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
                    </mover>
                  </mrow>
                </mrow>
              </msub>
            </mfrac>
          </mrow>
          <mo>)</mo>
        </mrow>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle {\text{DSR}}=\Phi \left({\frac {{\hat {SR}}^{*}-SR_{0}}{\sigma _{\hat {SR_{0}}}}}\right)}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/cbc43f0ca13919ad442ccc01f2f14776241a7344" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -3.171ex; width:25.935ex; height:7.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\text{DSR}}=\Phi \left({\frac {{\hat {SR}}^{*}-SR_{0}}{\sigma _{\hat {SR_{0}}}}}\right)}"></span></dd></dl>

<p id="mwAXM">This yields the probability that the observed Sharpe Ratio reflects true skill, not selection bias or overfitting. DSR is more accurate than methods based on <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Šidák_correction" title="Šidák correction" id="mwAXQ">Šidák correction</a>, because DSR takes into account the dispersion across trials, <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt105" id="mwAXU" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\mathbf{V}[\\hat{SR}_n]"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
          <mi mathvariant="bold">V</mi>
        </mrow>
        <mo stretchy="false">[</mo>
        <msub>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>
                  <mi>S</mi>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
              </mover>
            </mrow>
          </mrow>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mi>n</mi>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
        <mo stretchy="false">]</mo>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/982fa26cb95e80fad2f7571573cb453d4bf64296" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.838ex; width:7.795ex; height:3.343ex;" alt="{\displaystyle \mathbf {V} [{\hat {SR}}_{n}]}"></span>.</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="16" id="mwAXY" aria-labelledby="Confidence_and_power_of_the_Sharpe_ratio_under_multiple_testing"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Confidence_and_power_of_the_Sharpe_ratio_under_multiple_testing">Confidence and power of the Sharpe ratio under multiple testing</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=16" title="Edit section: Confidence and power of the Sharpe ratio under multiple testing"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAXc">To assess the significance of Sharpe Ratios under multiple testing, López de Prado (2018) derives closed-form expressions for the Type I and Type II errors.</p>

<section data-mw-section-id="17" id="mwAXg" aria-labelledby="Confidence"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Confidence">Confidence</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=17" title="Edit section: Confidence"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAXk">DSR is the probability of observing a Sharpe ratio less extreme than the estimated <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt106" id="mwAXo" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\hat{SR}^*"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msup>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>
                  <mi>S</mi>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
              </mover>
            </mrow>
          </mrow>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
          </mrow>
        </msup>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/feb56d887721483fd15981dd21c1d43a7726c775" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:4.317ex; height:3.009ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}"></span>, subject to <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt107" id="mwAXs" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"H_0:SR\\leq0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle H_{0}:SR\leq 0}">

          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>

        <mo>:</mo>

        <mi>R</mi>

  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/7918e7fba98cd63abc99174e6ee1de70df91b768" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:12.447ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle H_{0}:SR\leq 0}"></span> being true, where the multiple testing-adjusted baseline is <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt108" id="mwAXw" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>

        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span>. This can also be interpreted as the maximum confidence with which the null hypothesis can be rejected after observing <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt109" id="mwAX0" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\hat{SR}^*"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}">
  <semantics>

            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>

          </mrow>

    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/feb56d887721483fd15981dd21c1d43a7726c775" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:4.317ex; height:3.009ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}"></span>:<sup about="#mwt110" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-Power_14-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Power"}}"><a href="#cite_note-Power-14" id="mwAX4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAX8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYA">[</span>14<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<p id="mwAYU">where the standard deviation around the null hypothesis is:</p>

<p id="mwAYY"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt112" id="mwAYc" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\n\\sigma_{\\hat{SR_0}} = \\sqrt{\\frac{1 - \\hat{\\gamma}_3 SR_0 + \\frac{\\hat{\\gamma}_4 - 1}{4} SR_0^2 } {T-1}}\n"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \sigma _{\hat {SR_{0}}}={\sqrt {\frac {1-{\hat {\gamma }}_{3}SR_{0}+{\frac {{\hat {\gamma }}_{4}-1}{4}}SR_{0}^{2}}{T-1}}}}">
  <semantics>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="18" id="mwAYg" aria-labelledby="Power"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Power">Power</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=18" title="Edit section: Power"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAYk">The power of a test is the proportion of positives that are correctly identified. This is also known in <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning" title="Machine learning" id="mwAYo">machine learning</a> as the test's <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/True_positive_rate" title="True positive rate" class="mw-redirect" id="mwAYs">true positive rate</a> or recall, and sensitivity in medicine. Let <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt113" id="mwAYw" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_1"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{1}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>1</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{1}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/0648f4ffe3c318384cae7c5f63ea4cfdfade6bc3" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{1}}"></span> be the expected value of the alternative hypothesis, <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt114" id="mwAY0" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"H_1:SR>0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle H_{1}:SR>0}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msub>
          <mi>H</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>1</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
        <mo>:</mo>
        <mi>S</mi>
        <mi>R</mi>
        <mo>></mo>
        <mn>0</mn>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle H_{1}:SR>0}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/eb666725ef84f09011945f13bdd633ab84940fc4" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:12.447ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle H_{1}:SR>0}"></span>. For instance, this may be the average Sharpe ratio observed among strategies that have yielded positive excess returns. Then, the false negative rate (<span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt115" id="mwAY4" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\beta"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle \beta }">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>β<!-- β --></mi>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle \beta }</annotation>
  </semantics>

</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/50cbf597b2f73a22464f1fee6d541574cb044c55" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:5.335ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle 1-\beta }"></span>.</p>

</section><section data-mw-section-id="19" id="mwAao" aria-labelledby="Minimum_Track_Record_Length"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Minimum_Track_Record_Length">Minimum Track Record Length</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Deflated_Sharpe_ratio&action=edit&section=19" title="Edit section: Minimum Track Record Length"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAas">A related concept is the Minimum Track Record Length (MinTRL), which computes the minimum sample size <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt130" id="mwAaw" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"T"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle T}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>T</mi>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle T}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/ec7200acd984a1d3a3d7dc455e262fbe54f7f6e0" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:1.636ex; height:2.176ex;" alt="{\displaystyle T}"></span> needed such that a null hypothesis <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt131" id="mwAa0" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"SR_0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle SR_{0}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/04fbd508dffb79a364d55810b427483cba3afc09" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:4.317ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle SR_{0}}"></span> is rejected with confidence <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt132" id="mwAa4" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"DSR^*"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle DSR^{*}}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <mi>D</mi>
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msup>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
          </mrow>
        </msup>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle DSR^{*}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/c74b23554b670a5d347456776f4cd00c46efd430" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:6.242ex; height:2.343ex;" alt="{\displaystyle DSR^{*}}"></span>, given an observed <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt133" id="mwAa8" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\hat{SR}^*"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}">
  <semantics>

      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msup>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">

              <mover>
                <mrow>

                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>

</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/feb56d887721483fd15981dd21c1d43a7726c775" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:4.317ex; height:3.009ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}}"></span>.<sup about="#mwt134" class="mw-ref reference" id="cite_ref-:2_11-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref" data-mw="{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":2"}}"><a href="#cite_note-:2-11" id="mwAbA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAbE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbI">[</span>11<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbM">]</span></span></a></sup> Formally, the problem can be stated as</p>

<p id="mwAbQ"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt135" id="mwAbU" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\nMinTRL=min_T \\{P(\\hat{SR}<\\hat{SR}^*|H_0)=DSR^*\\}\n"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle MinTRL=min_{T}\{P({\hat {SR}}<{\hat {SR}}^{*}|H_{0})=DSR^{*}\}}">

        <mi>S</mi>
        <msup>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
          </mrow>
        </msup>
        <mo fence="false" stretchy="false">}</mo>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle MinTRL=min_{T}\{P({\hat {SR}}<{\hat {SR}}^{*}|H_{0})=DSR^{*}\}}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/55251849115bab4a9fade3aaa33f3c517ba76f55" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.838ex; width:47.879ex; height:3.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle MinTRL=min_{T}\{P({\hat {SR}}<{\hat {SR}}^{*}|H_{0})=DSR^{*}\}}"></span></p>

<p id="mwAbY">with solution</p>

<p id="mwAbc"><span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt136" id="mwAbg" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\nMinTRL=1+\\Bigl(1 - \\hat{\\gamma}_3 SR_0 + \\frac{\\hat{\\gamma}_4 - 1}{4} SR_0^2 \\Bigr)\\Bigl(\\frac{\\Phi(DSR^*)} {\\hat{SR}^*-SR_0} \\Bigr)^2\n"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle MinTRL=1+{\Bigl (}1-{\hat {\gamma }}_{3}SR_{0}+{\frac {{\hat {\gamma }}_{4}-1}{4}}SR_{0}^{2}{\Bigr )}{\Bigl (}{\frac {\Phi (DSR^{*})}{{\hat {SR}}^{*}-SR_{0}}}{\Bigr )}^{2}}">

                  <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">

          </mrow>
        </mrow>
        <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
          <mfrac>
            <mrow>

<p id="mwAbk">For example, given an observed annualized <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt137" id="mwAbo" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"\\hat{SR}^*=0.95"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}=0.95}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msup>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
              <mover>
                <mrow>
                  <mi>S</mi>
                  <mi>R</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mo stretchy="false">^<!-- ^ --></mo>
              </mover>
            </mrow>
          </mrow>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mo>∗<!-- ∗ --></mo>
          </mrow>
        </msup>
        <mo>=</mo>
        <mn>0.95</mn>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}=0.95}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/4d7b29226d41741b5dc9206c53c48e163573fe66" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.338ex; width:11.55ex; height:3.009ex;" alt="{\displaystyle {\hat {SR}}^{*}=0.95}"></span>, we need approximately 3 years worth of daily strategy returns in order to reject the null hypothesis <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline" typeof="mw:Extension/math" about="#mwt138" id="mwAbs" data-mw="{"name":"math","attrs":{},"body":{"extsrc":"H_0:SR_0=0"}}"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" alttext="{\displaystyle H_{0}:SR_{0}=0}">
  <semantics>
    <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
      <mstyle displaystyle="true" scriptlevel="0">
        <msub>
          <mi>H</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
        <mo>:</mo>
        <mi>S</mi>
        <msub>
          <mi>R</mi>
          <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
            <mn>0</mn>
          </mrow>
        </msub>
        <mo>=</mo>
        <mn>0</mn>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle H_{0}:SR_{0}=0}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/5a8c08f4132a6f1a41710b177d925f82c8e785a5" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -0.671ex; width:13.501ex; height:2.509ex;" alt="{\displaystyle H_{0}:SR_{0}=0}"></span> with confidence 95%. This provides mathematical support to the common expectation among investors that a <a rel="mw:WikiLink" href="//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hedge_fund" title="Hedge fund" id="mwAbw">hedge fund</a> must produce track records with a minimum length of 3 years, which may be reduced to 2 years for Sharpe ratios above 1.15. It is important to understand MinTRL as a minimum requirement, since this assumes a single trial (more trials will require longer track records).</p>
